description: How to Survive a Bear Attack, by Claire Cameron, finalist for the 2025 Governor General's Literary Award in Non-Fiction, investigates a rare bear attack in Algonquin Park while reflecting on her own battle with genetic cancer. name: The Life Cycle of the Common Octopus by Emma Knight - By the Lake Book Club
description: The Life Cycle of the Common Octopus, by Emma Knight, shortlisted for the 2025 Scotiabank Giller Prize, is a spellbinding debut about friendship, motherhood, first love and the secrets that shape us. name: Cowboys and Wallflowers Exhibition
description: Immerse in collages that blend iconic imagery and wit to challenge gender norms and domestic expectations. Despite shifting gender dynamics, her work highlights ongoing struggles over household roles. Each piece is meticulously handmade from vintage materials dating 1800s–1970s by Danielle Colle.

name: Immersion Festival
description: Join us for a celebration of Canadian cultural life for audiences and a sought-after performance and networking opportunity for renowned and aspiring artists, with three distinct stage performances, twelve workshops, a dance education roundtable, and community gatherings. name: 'Tis the Season Children's Art Exhibition
description:
Created by students from local schools, the works in this exhibition speak to the seasonal changes from autumn to winter, like snowfalls and long periods of darkness, and the cultural traditions they help shape.

name: Held by Anne Michaels - By the Lake Book Club
description: Held, by 2024 Scotiabank Giller Prize winner Anne Michaels, is a luminous, fragmented novel about love, memory, and the reverberations of war across generations.
